stem: The test scores for a class of 7 students are: 72, 75, 78, 80, 82, 85, 88. What is the median score?
explanation: To find the median, we need to identify the middle value when the data is arranged in order. The scores are already arranged in ascending order: 72, 75, 78, 80, 82, 85, 88. Since there are 7 values (an odd number), the median is the 4th value, which is the middle position. Counting from either end: the 4th value is 80. Therefore, the median score is 80.

distractorRationale.A: This represents choosing the 3rd value instead of the middle (4th) value in the ordered list.
distractorRationale.B: This is the correct answer - the middle value in the ordered list of 7 scores.
distractorRationale.C: This represents choosing the 5th value instead of the middle (4th) value in the ordered list.
distractorRationale.D: This represents choosing the 6th value, possibly confusing the median with a value closer to the upper end of the distribution.
